Sourcing Purpose: Photography prop — seed close-up and flat-lay
Spring Availability Risk: CRITICAL — fall crop; spring availability near zero. Verify May 7 before ordering. If unavailable skip prop seeds entirely.
Notes: If unavailable from all vendors: skip seed prop and use dried root instead. See row below.

Spring Availability Risk: NONE — dried root available year-round at retail
Notes: Highest-priority ginseng prop. Dried whole root communicates identification value far better than seeds. Purchase locally May 9 if possible. Mountain Rose Herbs 2-day shipping as fallback (order May 7 to ensure May 9 delivery).
Certified Organically Grown. 20-seed standard packet. Spring availability typical.

Goldenseal root (bright yellow color) is the most distinctive identification feature. Order in addition to seeds if delivery allows. If Johnny's lead time is too long, Mountain Rose Herbs 2-day ship is the reliable fallback.

Seeds spring-available from multiple sources. Potted live plant (~$8-12) from Strictly Medicinal is available mid-March through July — order if seeds are unavailable. Potted plant is a significant photography upgrade.

Common Name: Black Cohosh (live potted plant — optional upgrade)
Spring Availability Risk: LOW — plants ship mid-March through July
Notes: Optional — order only if seeds are unavailable OR if budget allows the upgrade. A live plant in habitat creates a stronger guide cover image than seeds alone.

Spring Availability Risk: HIGH — spring ephemeral with 1-week viable seed window; rhizome availability variable
Notes: Seeds viable only 1 week post-collection. Commercial spring seed is narrow. Rhizome (orange-red, distinctive) is the stronger identification prop. Check Prairie Moon for spring rhizome or bare-root availability first. If unavailable: use BHL botanical illustration (free) — Sanguinaria canadensis appears in multiple 19th-century herbal plates. No schedule impact.

Order Placed?: CRITICAL — EFN sold out as of May 2026. Check daily.
column25: EFN sold out. Check daily. If restocked: order immediately for 2-3 day delivery. Bulblets (Prairie Moon) and Etsy small-scale sellers are fallbacks. Double-dormancy seeds not germination-tested — prop photography use only.

Spring Availability Risk: MEDIUM — seasonal; available April-early May; check farmers market week of May 5
Notes: Fresh ramp leaves are the highest-value ramps prop — immediately recognizable to buyers. Available at specialty grocery stores and farmers markets in May. Check availability week of May 5. If unavailable: use iNaturalist CC-BY archive photo (spring 2020-2025 observations) for leaf imagery in guide.

Order Placed?: NONE — always in stock from multiple vendors
column25: Widely available year-round. No availability risk.

Spring Availability Risk: NONE — typically in stock at hardware stores in May
Notes: Highly recommended over seeds alone. Live flowering Monarda fistulosa is typically stocked at hardware store garden centers in May. A live plant creates a far more compelling guide cover image. Purchase locally May 9 for minimal cost.

Sourcing Purpose: Guide interior photography — full plant / habitat / flower views (primary photo source)
Quantity: 5-8 Research Grade CC-BY observations per species
Notes: CRITICAL: Filter URL must include photo_license=cc-by — default is CC-BY-NC (prohibits commercial use). Attribution required: 'Photo: [Observer Name], iNaturalist, CC BY 4.0'. Log each selected observation: species, observer name, iNat URL, intended guide section. Target 5 angles per species: full plant, leaf, flower/fruit, root/rhizome, seed/seed pod.

Sourcing Purpose: Guide interior photography — heritage botanical illustrations (guaranteed fallback)
Notes: Pre-1928 material is public domain — no attribution required (citing BHL is good practice). Key resource: 'Ginseng and Goldenseal Growers Handbook 1912' at biodiversitylibrary.org/item/116021 has engraved illustrations of both species. Search Latin name in BHL main search + Flickr. Download highest resolution available.

Sourcing Purpose: Guide interior photography — institutional macro photos (Tier 1 partnership)
Primary Price: Free for eligible macro use ($500+/image for full scenes)
Spring Availability Risk: MEDIUM — response not guaranteed; free for macro-format only
Notes: Policy: single-subject close-cropped macro photos = free commercial use. Full garden scenes = $500+/image. Email pr@mobot.org by May 7. Template in phase-2-production-execution-plan.md Section 2. Response expected 3-7 business days. Do not delay production waiting for response — Tiers 2 and 3 are sufficient.

Sourcing Purpose: Guide interior photography — institutional macro photos (Tier 1 partnership)
Spring Availability Risk: MEDIUM — Appalachian-native species focus matches their collection
Notes: Contact via contact form at ncbg.unc.edu. Focus request on black cohosh and ramps in their Appalachian native plant collection. Response timeline variable. Do not delay production waiting for response.

Cash event: May 8-9. One-time outlay; no recurring cost. Breakdown: prop seeds $27-71 (all species combined) + retail herb specimens $27-45 (ginseng root, goldenseal root, ramps leaves) + live potted plants $4-8 (Wild Bergamot). Does not include photography equipment (assumed on hand) or print shop run ($15-20 for color prints). All prop material consumed in May 10-11 shoot — no inventory carried forward. Write to COGS at first sale.
